# Thrips

by Nebula Haze
## What Does Cannabis Thrip Damage Look Like?
Adult thrips are small, fast-moving insects, while young thrips look like tiny unmoving pale worms on the leaves. In fact, thrips can come in many forms, from wormy nymphs to dark or golden winged insects, depending on the stage of life and where you live.
They pierce cannabis leaves with their mouths and suck out all the good stuff, leaving shiny (sometimes people think it looks slimy), silver or bronze spots wherever the leaves were bitten. The spots are bigger and more irregularly shaped than the bites left from spider mites. If it goes on too long the affected leaves may start dying.
Examples of thrips damage on marijuana leaves (irregular silver or bronze spots)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/marijuana-leaf-closeup-thrip-damage.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/marijuana-thrips-spots-leaves-damage.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/marijuana-thrips-spots-leaves-damage-closeup.jpg)
Although it doesn’t really look like it in pictures, in real life thrip damage has been described as looking like “dried spit” or tiny snail trails.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/marijuana-thrips-leaf-damage-silvery-spots.jpg)  
_(thrip leaf damage pics by theMallacht)_
_[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/cannabis-leaf-closeup-thrip-damage.jpg)_
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/cannabis-leaf-damage-from-thrips.jpg)
You may see small thrip nymphs on the cannabis leaves. The nymphs look tubular and worm-like. In this picture, you can also see the thrip damage on the surrounding parts of the leaf.
The red arrow points at the wormy thrip nymph.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/cannabis-thrips-closeup-worm-larvae-spots-on-leaves-sm.jpg)
Here’s a picture of an adult thrip on a finger for scale. Even the adult thrips are tiny!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/thrip-on-finger-for-scale.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/dark-thrip-adult-closeup-marijuana-leaf.jpg)
They can appear dark colored like the ones above, but also yellow, transparent or golden
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/cannabis-thrips-pest.jpg)
They can appear with or without wings, depending on their stage of life
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/yellow-thrip-closeup-cannabis.jpg)
In their “nymph” (juvenile) form, thrips appear pale, fat and almost wormy from afar
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/thrips-nymph-marijuana.jpg)
A closeup of another baby thrip in “nymph” form
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/cannabis-thrips-closeup2.jpg)
A Thrip nymph on a cannabis leaf – I hope this helps show you how  _tiny_ they are.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/thrip-nymph-worm-on-cannabis-leaf.jpg)
A thrip nymph looks tubular and worm-like, unlike an [aphid](https://www.growweedeasy.com/bugs-pests-symptoms-marijuana-grow#aphids) nymph which looks like a tiny white bug
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/worm-nymph-thrip-on-marijuana-leaf.jpg)
**Proven Thrip Remedies**
**1.) Insecticidal soap**
Fatty acid salts or insecticidal soaps can be a good choice against thrips. They weaken the outer shell of thrips but are safe to use on your plants and they don’t leave much of a residue.
With soaps, coverage is very important as it does not stay on your plant for long, so follow-up applications may be necessary. Although this is considered safe, avoid getting any on your buds!
[](https://www.amazon.com/Garden-Safe-Insecticidal-Soap-Ready/dp/B0BDVCCKKT?ie=UTF8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=44d26da8ba029e648afe81d485bd3c35&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**2.) Neem Oil**
[Neem Oil](https://www.amazon.com/Dyna-Gro-NEM-008-Pure-Natural-Polish/dp/B0012BGXVQ/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&qid=1533668748&sr=8-17&keywords=neem+oil&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=09cd923d74159a96951622aef9d4f8a8&language=en_US) will leave an unpleasant taste/smell on buds when used to treat flowering plants, so don’t let this stuff get near your buds! There’s also some evidence Neem oil may be [harmful to humans](http://www.ajnr.org/content/31/7/E60.full) so use with care! That being said, Neem oil is an all-natural remedy that is very effective against many different types of bugs and mold. You will need a [mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-1002-48-Ounce-Sprayer-Multi-purpose/dp/B00002N8OB/ref=as_li_ss_tl?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_i=B00002N8OB&pd_rd_r=W4A5MVZWMFWH74AT55F1&pd_rd_w=UPVXy&pd_rd_wg=EwzKa&psc=1&refRID=W4A5MVZWMFWH74AT55F1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a160ef9049bbd8e2fce0dda65c9e5181) (also called a “One-Hand Pressure Sprayer”) to spray all the leaves evenly since neem oil and water can separate easily.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/neem-oil-cannabis-insecticide.jpg)[](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-1002-48-Ounce-Sprayer-Multi-purpose/dp/B00002N8OB?crid=1KOB888PSUVXP&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.KUeBQh2OB2oMO2EaQiXegm5B6gxoI9_rkWvYVVB7vGJPBtkZjdIkuy7O9fCIfRpe4feZQIJqt5vySXCTCGclZf6awgPI6-Cwht6Bar4cGaKfeYL-rGRtxy3W-w-OwoN3JXbNJ7qZ1QLHo1zweWPiC_BQKuuO7rQ3QQ3yBs8i_trbsIxdg7GVaPODfMv6L20tFLh6Oqg-UQvZ3qOfrNISx9XIc3eDzPJARD3uhSZ0Wln-GmiPB1x3a_a7e5oJ3w-amKaBV9K5dGoxsA3Ig2TKQdjDv9n-cByd02JsZHtzdME.iDwoHbwFaHKfBOY_ltl1rUAxeQ08ciyXqRhZKbDSGCM&dib_tag=se&keywords=chapin%2Bsprayer&qid=1709076120&sprefix=chapin%2B%2Caps%2C198&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e739cffbc4ce965fe96ef5c441dbadaf&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**3.) Spinosad Products (not for[commercial growers](https://www.growweedeasy.com/is-spinosad-safe-for-growing-weed))**
Spinosad products are organic and, unlike many other thrip pesticides,**completely harmless to pets, children, and plants**. Unlike many insecticides, you can spray spinosad heavily on leaves and roots with basically no negative effects. Spinosad products can be used directly to kill thrips on contact, but can also be used when watering plants to systematically kill thrips via the roots. Spinosad is also effective at fighting caterpillars, spider mites, and many other marijuana pests.
It can be used both as a topical spray and can also be used directly at the roots. Spinosad is an organic insecticide made from the fermentation of a specific soil bacteria (actinomycete Saccharopolyspora spinosa) and kills thrips via ingestion or contact by affecting the insect nervous system. Spinosad can be a good choice for organic and outdoor growers, because it is very toxic to thrips, but is less toxic to many beneficial insects.
Note: Most spinosad products are effective for only about 24 hours after being mixed with water, so only mix as much as you will need per application. Anything left over will be waste.
WARNING: [Commercial growers click here!](https://www.growweedeasy.com/is-spinosad-safe-for-growing-weed)
**Recommended****:**[Monterey Garden Insect Spray with Spinosad](https://www.amazon.com/Monterey-LG6150-Garden-Spinosad-Concentrate/dp/B000BWY3OQ/ref=as_li_ss_tl?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1525732651&sr=8-1&keywords=Monterey+Garden+Insect+Spray+with+Spinosad&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=0bbb77552e20bf98254d769721775fa3)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/monteray-garden-insect-spray-160.jpg)[](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-1002-48-Ounce-Sprayer-Multi-purpose/dp/B00002N8OB?crid=1KOB888PSUVXP&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.KUeBQh2OB2oMO2EaQiXegm5B6gxoI9_rkWvYVVB7vGJPBtkZjdIkuy7O9fCIfRpe4feZQIJqt5vySXCTCGclZf6awgPI6-Cwht6Bar4cGaKfeYL-rGRtxy3W-w-OwoN3JXbNJ7qZ1QLHo1zweWPiC_BQKuuO7rQ3QQ3yBs8i_trbsIxdg7GVaPODfMv6L20tFLh6Oqg-UQvZ3qOfrNISx9XIc3eDzPJARD3uhSZ0Wln-GmiPB1x3a_a7e5oJ3w-amKaBV9K5dGoxsA3Ig2TKQdjDv9n-cByd02JsZHtzdME.iDwoHbwFaHKfBOY_ltl1rUAxeQ08ciyXqRhZKbDSGCM&dib_tag=se&keywords=chapin%2Bsprayer&qid=1709076120&sprefix=chapin%2B%2Caps%2C198&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e739cffbc4ce965fe96ef5c441dbadaf&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**4.) Pyrethrins**
Pyrethrin based insecticides are not very toxic for humans and degrade quickly, which is why they’re commonly recommended for vegetable gardens. You will need a [mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-1002-48-Ounce-Sprayer-Multi-purpose/dp/B00002N8OB/ref=as_li_ss_tl?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_i=B00002N8OB&pd_rd_r=W4A5MVZWMFWH74AT55F1&pd_rd_w=UPVXy&pd_rd_wg=EwzKa&psc=1&refRID=W4A5MVZWMFWH74AT55F1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a160ef9049bbd8e2fce0dda65c9e5181) (also called a “One-Hand Pressure Sprayer”) to spray all the leaves evenly.
Pyrethrin products break down quickly, over the course of just a day or two. The major problem with them is they are very toxic to bees. Although cannabis plants generally don’t attract a lot of bees, please use this as a last resort, and also try to use it right after the sun goes down because bees sleep at night. This lets it start to break down before they wake up.
**Use pyrethrin products when the sun goes down! Save the bees!**
A popular example is [Bonide 857 Pyrethrin Spray Concentrate](https://www.amazon.com/Bonide-Products-857-Pyrethrin-Concentrate/dp/B0035HA0AK/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&qid=1525732549&sr=8-3&keywords=Bonide+857+Pyrethrin+Spray+Concentrate&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=572da4e2c037ebae94150f3f6ce020f9). Use 3 tablespoons of concentrate per gallon of water.
[](https://www.amazon.com/Bonide-Products-857-Pyrethrin-Concentrate/dp/B0035HA0AK?ie=UTF8&qid=1525732549&sr=8-3&keywords=Bonide+857+Pyrethrin+Spray+Concentrate&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=6de932fc00a5392f2adc6bdffb89ea92&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)[](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-1002-48-Ounce-Sprayer-Multi-purpose/dp/B00002N8OB?crid=1KOB888PSUVXP&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.KUeBQh2OB2oMO2EaQiXegm5B6gxoI9_rkWvYVVB7vGJPBtkZjdIkuy7O9fCIfRpe4feZQIJqt5vySXCTCGclZf6awgPI6-Cwht6Bar4cGaKfeYL-rGRtxy3W-w-OwoN3JXbNJ7qZ1QLHo1zweWPiC_BQKuuO7rQ3QQ3yBs8i_trbsIxdg7GVaPODfMv6L20tFLh6Oqg-UQvZ3qOfrNISx9XIc3eDzPJARD3uhSZ0Wln-GmiPB1x3a_a7e5oJ3w-amKaBV9K5dGoxsA3Ig2TKQdjDv9n-cByd02JsZHtzdME.iDwoHbwFaHKfBOY_ltl1rUAxeQ08ciyXqRhZKbDSGCM&dib_tag=se&keywords=chapin%2Bsprayer&qid=1709076120&sprefix=chapin%2B%2Caps%2C198&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e739cffbc4ce965fe96ef5c441dbadaf&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**5.) Diatomaceous Earth**
[Diatomaceous Earth](https://www.amazon.com/HARRIS-Diatomaceous-Crawling-Insect-Included/dp/B082LRSCV8?dchild=1&keywords=Diatomaceous+Earth&qid=1628982195&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExU1IwN0VLT0kwRVFWJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMTQ5ODg1MTYxMkdOMkJLVkJIRyZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwOTI2NDU0MU9LUU4xODcwTzZJUyZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=19a474a2e8903b9e70c3852e43294f5e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is basically fossil dust that can be sprinkled on the top of your soil, and anywhere else in your room (window sills, doorways, etc). This powder-like substance is harmless to mammals and plants but is incredibly sharp at the microscopic level. Therefore it will tear and dehydrate thrips on physical contact. Mixing some of this in with the topsoil of your plants, and spreading the powder around the bottom of the tent near the plants can actually cut down thrip numbers relatively quickly.
Sprinkle your soil and the area around your plants with [Diatomaceous Earth](https://www.amazon.com/HARRIS-Diatomaceous-Crawling-Insect-Included/dp/B082LRSCV8?dchild=1&keywords=Diatomaceous+Earth&qid=1628982195&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExU1IwN0VLT0kwRVFWJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMTQ5ODg1MTYxMkdOMkJLVkJIRyZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwOTI2NDU0MU9LUU4xODcwTzZJUyZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=361beb7630844c7f8bd3ade8e314b858&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) to help kill any bugs that crawl over it
[](https://www.amazon.com/HARRIS-Diatomaceous-Crawling-Insect-Included/dp/B082LRSCV8?dchild=1&keywords=Diatomaceous+Earth&qid=1628982195&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExU1IwN0VLT0kwRVFWJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMTQ5ODg1MTYxMkdOMkJLVkJIRyZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwOTI2NDU0MU9LUU4xODcwTzZJUyZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=361beb7630844c7f8bd3ade8e314b858&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
* * *

## Plant Symptoms
  * [Bronze or brown patches](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/bronze-spots-or-patches)
  * [Brown or slimy roots](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/roots-are-brown-smelly-or-slimy)
  * [Brown or yellow leaf tips/edges](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/brown-yellow-or-burnt-edges-or-tips)
  * [Buds dying](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/buds-dying)
  * [Buds look odd](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/buds-look-odd)
  * [Bugs are visible](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/i-see-bugs)
  * [Curling or clawing leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/curling-or-clawing-leaves)
  * [Dark leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/dark-green)
  * [Drooping plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/drooping-plant)
  * [Holes in leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/holes-in-leaves)
  * [Mold or powder](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/mold-or-white-powder)
  * [Pink or purple on leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/pink-or-purple-on-leaves)
  * [Red stems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/red-stems)
  * [Shiny or smooth leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/shiny-or-smooth-leaves)
  * [Spots or markings](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/spots-or-markings-on-leaves)
  * [Twisted growth](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/twisted-growth)
  * [Webbing](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/webbing)
  * [Wilting leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/wilting-leaves)
  * [Yellow between leaf veins](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/yellow-between-leaf-veins)
  * [Yellow leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/yellow-leaves)

This page is part of our Plant Doctor series. You can use our tool to filter by symptom and help [diagnose your plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures).

GrowWeedEasy.com was [founded in 2010](https://www.growweedeasy.com/about) by Nebula Haze and Sirius Fourside. Featured in _High Times_ , Ed Rosenthal’s books, _Cannabis Business Times_ , and _Skunk Magazine_ , GrowWeedEasy.com is the **#1 free resource in the world** for learning how to grow cannabis at home. Our simple step-by-step tutorials make it easy for anyone to grow great weed. **[Sign up for our free newsletter](https://www.growweedeasy.com/message/news)** to get grow tips delivered every Sunday at 4:20 AM.
